name: buyback_principle
observations: **The Buyback Principle**: Don't hire to grow your business. Hire to buy back your time.
Core concept: Use every resource to buy back your time, then reinvest that time in higher-value tasks that energize you and make more money.
Goal: Spend majority of time in the Production Quadrant—doing what lights you up and makes you money.
**The Buyback Loop**: Audit → Transfer → Fill. Continuously audit your time, transfer low-value tasks to others, fill your freed time with high-value tasks that light you up.
**Buyback Rate Formula**: What your company pays you ÷ 8,000 = Your Buyback Rate. This is the hourly rate you can afford to pay someone else to do a task.
Example: If your company pays you $200,000/year, your Buyback Rate is $25/hour. Never do a task you could outsource for less than your Buyback Rate.

name: drip_matrix
observations: **DRIP Matrix**: 4 quadrants for evaluating tasks by money (low to high) and energy (drains to energizes).
**D - Delegation Quadrant** (low money, drains energy): Administrative work, billing, invoicing, email, travel arrangements. Transfer these ASAP.
**R - Replacement Quadrant** (high money, drains energy): Sales, marketing, delivery, team management. Important but draining. Use the Replacement Ladder to systematically offload.
**I - Investment Quadrant** (low money, energizes): Hobbies, health, relationships, industry collaborations, personal development. Always keep these—investments in yourself.
**P - Production Quadrant** (high money, energizes): Your genius zone. Tasks you excel at, enjoy, and that add highest value. Spend majority of time here.
**80% done by someone else is 100% freaking awesome.** - Dan Martell

name: replacement_ladder
observations: **Replacement Ladder**: 5 rungs to systematically offload responsibilities. Follow in order.
**Rung 1 - Admin**: Key Hire: Admin Assistant. Feeling: Stuck. Ownership: Inbox and Calendar. First hire every entrepreneur must make.
**Rung 2 - Delivery**: Key Hire: Head of Delivery. Feeling: Stalled. Ownership: Onboarding and Support. Your core product/service delivery.
**Rung 3 - Marketing**: Key Hire: Head of Marketing. Feeling: Friction. Ownership: Campaigns and Traffic. End the feast-or-famine cycle.
**Rung 4 - Sales**: Key Hire: Sales Representative. Feeling: Freedom. Ownership: Calls and Follow-Up. Offload when necessary to continue growth.
**Rung 5 - Leadership**: Key Hires: All Levels of Leadership. Feeling: Flow. Collaboration: Strategy and Leadership. Empire-builder level.
At each rung: Identify your feeling → Make key hire → Transfer ownership → Move to next rung.

name: 10-80-10_rule
observations: **10-80-10 Rule**: When you want to stay involved but don't need to do all the work.
**First 10%**: You do the initial setup—set the direction, vision, and expectations.
**Middle 80%**: Delegate to someone else to execute.
**Final 10%**: You review and add your magic touch.
Use this for creative work, product development, or any task where you want quality control without doing everything.
Example: Design the cake, let someone else bake it, then decorate it yourself.

name: camcorder_method
observations: **Camcorder Method**: Record yourself doing tasks to create training videos without spending additional time training.
Process: Hit record → Perform the task while narrating what you're doing → Drop video into a Playbook.
**Three recordings rule**: Record yourself doing the same task 3 times to capture all variations and edge cases.
Tools: Use Loom, Zoom, or any screen recording software.
Key: Talk through what you're doing AS you do it. This captures all the nuance.
Result: Train others without being present. 80% done by someone else is 100% awesome.

name: time_assassins
observations: **5 Time Assassins**: Psychological patterns that kill entrepreneur productivity. Each stems from chaos addiction.
**1. The Staller**: Drags feet on big decisions. Sabotages success by hesitating on opportunities.
**2. The Speed Demon**: Makes rapid, hasty decisions—hires first candidate, picks first platform. Keeps making same mistakes.
**3. The Supervisor**: Micromanages, does work for employees, creates dependency. 'Only I can do it right' mentality.
**4. The Saver**: Hoards money instead of reinvesting. Will burn out to save a few bucks.
**5. The Self-Medicator**: Turns to vices (food, alcohol) to celebrate wins OR escape pain. Sabotages next day's productivity.
Solution: Identify your current assassin. Carry a reminder. Recognize it when it tries to pop up during decisions.
